# aioice

{{ rtd }} {{ pypi-v }} {{ pypi-pyversions }} {{ pypi-l }} {{ pypi-wheel }} {{ tests }} {{ codecov }}

## What is `aioice`?

`aioice` is a library for Interactive Connectivity Establishment (RFC 5245)
in Python. It is built on top of `asyncio`, Python's standard asynchronous
I/O framework.

Interactive Connectivity Establishment (ICE) is useful for applications that
establish peer-to-peer UDP data streams, as it facilitates NAT traversal.
Typical usecases include SIP and WebRTC.

To learn more about `aioice` please [read the documentation].

## Example

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python

import asyncio
import aioice

async def connect_using_ice():
    connection = aioice.Connection(ice_controlling=True)

    # gather local candidates
    await connection.gather_candidates()

    # send your information to the remote party using your signaling method
    send_local_info(
        connection.local_candidates,
        connection.local_username,
        connection.local_password)

    # receive remote information using your signaling method
    remote_candidates, remote_username, remote_password = get_remote_info()

    # perform ICE handshake
    for candidate in remote_candidates:
        await connection.add_remote_candidate(candidate)
    await connection.add_remote_candidate(None)
    connection.remote_username = remote_username
    connection.remote_password = remote_password
    await connection.connect()

    # send and receive data
    await connection.sendto(b'1234', 1)
    data, component = await connection.recvfrom()

    # close connection
    await connection.close()

asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(connect_using_ice())
```

documentation: flake8: noqa

functions.name: aioice.candidate.candidate_foundation
documentation: See RFC 5245 - 4.1.1.3. Computing Foundations

functions.name: aioice.candidate.candidate_priority
documentation: See RFC 5245 - 4.1.2.1. Recommended Formula

classes.name: aioice.candidate.Candidate

methods.name: aioice.candidate.Candidate.from_sdp
documentation:
Parse a {class}`Candidate` from SDP.

```python
Candidate.from_sdp(
 '6815297761 1 udp 659136 1.2.3.4 31102 typ host generation 0')
```

methods.name: aioice.candidate.Candidate.to_sdp
documentation: Return a string representation suitable for SDP.

methods.name: aioice.candidate.Candidate.can_pair_with
documentation:
A local candidate is paired with a remote candidate if and only if
the two candidates have the same component ID and have the same IP
address version.
An ICE candidate.

functions.name: aioice.stun.padding_length
documentation: STUN message attributes are padded to a 4-byte boundary.

functions.name: aioice.stun.parse_message
documentation:
Parses a STUN message.

If the `integrity_key` parameter is given, the message's HMAC will be verified.

methods.name: aioice.stun.Message.add_message_integrity
documentation:
Add MESSAGE-INTEGRITY and FINGERPRINT attributes to the message.

This must be the last step before sending out the message.

functions.name: aioice.mdns.create_mdns_protocol
documentation:
Using a single socket works fine on Linux, but on OS X we need to use
separate sockets for sending and receiving.

methods.name: aioice.mdns.MDnsProtocol.close
documentation: custom

functions.name: aioice.turn.create_turn_endpoint
documentation: Create datagram connection relayed over TURN.

methods.name: aioice.turn.TurnClientMixin.connect
documentation: Create a TURN allocation.

methods.name: aioice.turn.TurnClientMixin.delete
documentation: Delete the TURN allocation.

methods.name: aioice.turn.TurnClientMixin.refresh
documentation: Periodically refresh the TURN allocation.

methods.name: aioice.turn.TurnClientMixin.request
documentation: Execute a STUN transaction and return the response.

methods.name: aioice.turn.TurnClientMixin.request_with_retry
documentation:
Execute a STUN transaction and return the response.

On recoverable errors it will retry the request.

methods.name: aioice.turn.TurnClientMixin.send_data
documentation: Send data to a remote host via the TURN server.

methods.name: aioice.turn.TurnClientMixin.send_stun
documentation: Send a STUN message to the TURN server.

classes.name: aioice.turn.TurnClientTcpProtocol
documentation: Protocol for handling TURN over TCP.

classes.name: aioice.turn.TurnClientUdpProtocol
documentation: Protocol for handling TURN over UDP.

classes.name: aioice.turn.TurnTransport

methods.name: aioice.turn.TurnTransport.close
documentation:
Close the transport.

After the TURN allocation has been deleted, the protocol's
`connection_lost()` method will be called with None as its argument.

methods.name: aioice.turn.TurnTransport.get_extra_info
documentation:
Return optional transport information.

- `'related_address'`: the related address
- `'sockname'`: the relayed address

methods.name: aioice.turn.TurnTransport.sendto
documentation:
Sends the `data` bytes to the remote peer given `addr`.

This will bind a TURN channel as necessary.
Behaves like a Datagram transport, but uses a TURN allocation.

functions.name: aioice.ice.candidate_pair_priority
documentation: See RFC 5245 - 5.7.2. Computing Pair Priority and Ordering Pairs

functions.name: aioice.ice.get_host_addresses
documentation: Get local IP addresses.

functions.name: aioice.ice.server_reflexive_candidate
documentation: Query STUN server to obtain a server-reflexive candidate.

functions.name: aioice.ice.sort_candidate_pairs
documentation: Sort a list of candidate pairs.

functions.name: aioice.ice.validate_remote_candidate
documentation: Check the remote candidate is supported.

class_variables.name: aioice.ice.TransportPolicy.ALL
documentation: All ICE candidates will be considered.

class_variables.name: aioice.ice.TransportPolicy.RELAY
documentation:
Only ICE candidates whose IP addresses are being relayed,
such as those being passed through a STUN or TURN server,
will be considered.

documentation: custom

documentation: Execute a STUN transaction and return the response.

methods.name: aioice.ice.StunProtocol.send_stun
documentation: Send a STUN message.

classes.name: aioice.ice.Connection

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.local_candidates
documentation: Local candidates, automatically set by {meth}`gather_candidates`.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.remote_candidates
documentation:
Remote candidates, which you need to populate using
{meth}`add_remote_candidate`.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.add_remote_candidate
documentation:
Add a remote candidate or signal end-of-candidates.

To signal end-of-candidates, pass `None`.

```
param remote_candidate

A  instance or .
```

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.gather_candidates
documentation:
Gather local candidates.

You **must** call this coroutine before calling {meth}`connect`.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.get_default_candidate
documentation:
Get the default local candidate for the specified component.

```
param component

The component whose default candidate is requested.
```

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.connect
documentation:
Perform ICE handshake.

This coroutine returns if a candidate pair was successfuly nominated
and raises an exception otherwise.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.close
documentation: Close the connection.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.get_event
documentation:
Return the next `ConnectionEvent` or `None` if the connection is
already closed.

This method may only be called once at a time.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.recv
documentation:
Receive the next datagram.

The return value is a `bytes` object representing the data received.

If the connection is not established, a `ConnectionError` is raised.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.recvfrom
documentation:
Receive the next datagram.

The return value is a `(bytes, component)` tuple where `bytes` is a
bytes object representing the data received and `component` is the
component on which the data was received.

If the connection is not established, a `ConnectionError` is raised.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.send
documentation:
Send a datagram on the first component.

If the connection is not established, a `ConnectionError` is raised.

```
param data

The data to be sent.
```

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.sendto
documentation:
Send a datagram on the specified component.

If the connection is not established, a `ConnectionError` is raised.

```
param data

The data to be sent.

param component

The component on which to send the data.
```

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.set_selected_pair
documentation:
Force the selected candidate pair.

If the remote party does not support ICE, you should using this
instead of calling {meth}`connect`.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.build_request
documentation: private

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.check_incoming
documentation: Handle a succesful incoming check.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.check_start
documentation: Starts a check.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.check_start_task
documentation: Starts a check in a task, unless already started.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.check_state
documentation: Updates the state of a check.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection._find_pair
documentation: Find a candidate pair in the check list.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection._prune_components
documentation:
Remove components for which the remote party did not provide any candidates.

This can only be determined after end-of-candidates.

methods.name: aioice.ice.Connection.query_consent
documentation: Periodically check consent (RFC 7675).

An ICE connection for a single media stream.

```
param ice_controlling

Whether the local peer has the controlling role.

param components

The number of components.

param stun_server

The address of the STUN server or .

param turn_server

The address of the TURN server or .

param turn_username

The username for the TURN server.

param turn_password

The password for the TURN server.

param turn_ssl

Whether to use TLS for the TURN server.

param turn_transport

The transport for TURN server,  or .

param use_ipv4

Whether to use IPv4 candidates.

param use_ipv6

Whether to use IPv6 candidates.

param transport_policy

Transport policy.
```
